title: psyllium indicum subsp. orientale
intro: Psyllium indicum subsp. orientale, also known as Indian psyllium or isabgol, is a plant that belongs to the plantago family and is native to the Indian subcontinent. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Psyllium indicum subsp. orientale include its ability to help manage digestive issues, such as constipation, diarrhea, and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as its potential to help lower cholesterol levels and blood sugar levels. Herbal preparations of the plant include capsules, powders, and teas, which can be used to support gut health and overall well-being.
